title: swift-universal common-process needs swift-subprocess 0.3+/0.5 API migration — blocks CommonShell retrofit on all consuming workstream Packages
description:
Per operator 2026-05-31: 'use common-process, common-shell and common-cli where appropriate too' + 'NOOOOOO you should not make drift' + 'subprocess is all the way up to 0.5... so going back seems wrong....'

ROOT CAUSE: swift-universal common-process source at `private/universal/domain/build/spm/common-process/sources/common-process-runners/SubprocessRunner.swift` uses swift-subprocess 0.2.x APIs (non-generic `Execution` + escape-from-closure value storage + old `TeardownStep` enum shape). swift-subprocess 0.3.0 (2026-01-23) introduced THREE breaking changes that common-process source has not been migrated for:

  1. `Execution` became `Execution<Input: InputProtocol, Output: OutputProtocol, Error: OutputProtocol>` — SubprocessRunner.swift:10 `var execution: Execution?` no longer compiles.
  2. Added contract: 'The value is valid only for the duration of the closure. Don't let the execution value escape the closure.' — SubprocessExecHolder stores Execution OUTSIDE the closure scope, which is the new anti-pattern.
  3. `TeardownStep` changed from enum to `public struct TeardownStep` with internal Storage; the `gracefulShutDown` parameter renamed from `allowedDurationToNextStep:` to `allowedDuration:` — SubprocessRunner.swift:172 `.gracefulShutDown(allowedDurationToNextStep: .milliseconds(200))` no longer compiles.
  Plus a likely Signal API change at SubprocessRunner.swift:202 `exec.send(signal: .interrupt, ...)`.

VERSION TIMELINE (from `swift-subprocess` git tags):
  - 0.1   → 2025-09-04
  - 0.2   → 2025-10-15
  - 0.2.1 → 2025-10-21 ← LAST WORKING version for common-process current source
  - 0.3   → 2026-01-23 ← FIRST BREAKING (Execution generic + escape-closure contract)
  - 0.4   → 2026-03-24
  - 0.5   → 2026-05-29 ← LATEST (2 days ago)

WHY chained creative-selection build resolves 0.5.0 while standalone common-process resolves 0.2.1: common-process's `from: "0.2.0"` allows the full 0.x range; SwiftPM's version-graph solver picks DIFFERENT versions based on the overall dependency graph context. Standalone gets 0.2.1; transitively through workstream consumer the resolver picks 0.5.0.

WHY PIN-TO-0.2.X IS NOT THE RIGHT FIX: Operator's diagnostic — 'subprocess is all the way up to 0.5... so going back seems wrong.' Pinning common-process to 0.2.x skips 4 months of swift-subprocess fixes (0.2.1 → 0.5 = 7 months gap). The RIGHT fix is to migrate common-process source to handle the swift-subprocess 0.3+/0.5 APIs:
  - Refactor SubprocessExecHolder to NOT store Execution outside the closure scope; carry typed type-erased closures or use a generic SubprocessExecHolder<Input, Output, Error>.
  - Update `.gracefulShutDown(allowedDurationToNextStep:)` → `.gracefulShutDown(allowedDuration:)`.
  - Migrate signal-send call to the new Signal API.
  - Update tests + bench-support if they exercise these surfaces.

SCOPE OF UPSTREAM WORK: substantial — multiple files in common-process, likely tests too, possibly common-process-foundation-runner if it shares shapes. Out of scope for the 2026-05-31 turn that surfaced this. Tracked here for swift-universal collective maintainer's next focused turn.

WHEN UPSTREAM IS FIXED: re-apply the CommonShellArguments retrofit (typed @OptionGroup) to creative-selection's .cli + .clia, plus the 4 sibling workstream Packages (spawn-software, maintain-software, creative-selection-loop, clia-rpg-spawn-run) + md@swift-universal.cli + workstream@wrkstrm-core.cli + agent-timeline-cli + digikoma-*-cli + harness@takumi-org.cli. The CommonShellArguments composition itself (typed --verbose / --working-directory / --outputs via @OptionGroup) is verified working when the chained build resolves swift-subprocess 0.2.x — the substrate-doctrine application is correct, only blocked by upstream tech debt.

Swift toolchain at time of verification: Apple Swift 6.3 (swiftlang-6.3.0.123.5 clang-2100.0.123.102) on arm64-apple-macosx26.0.
blockerSource: swift-universal common-process — swift-subprocess 0.3+/0.5 API migration not done

breakingChanges: 'Don't let Execution escape the closure' contract added
evidence.consumerImpact: all common-shell + common-process consumers in substrate

rejectedFix.approach: Pin swift-subprocess to '0.2.0 ..< 0.3.0' in common-process Package.swift
rejectedFix.rejectionReason: subprocess is all the way up to 0.5... so going back seems wrong
